mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and supports equity multiples, so lower policy rates should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the cut is part of an easing cycle rather than a recession scare.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS falls for three consecutive monthly observations, or if the signal's rank IC stays below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so when unemployment rises sharply it should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after a 0.3pp unemployment rise is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve, the economy shows broad retail demand supported by job creation, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not higher when both RSAFS and PAYEMS are above their 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: A broad-based rise in INDPRO reflects stronger current output and inventory replenishment, which should support cyclical equities and higher S&P 500 returns over the next week as surprise growth data are absorbed quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-forward returns after positive INDPRO momentum are not above zero, or if the sign of the effect flips for more than one third of rolling 1-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST signals housing demand deterioration and tighter downstream activity, so when housing starts weaken materially the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns after a >5% two-month HOUST decline are not negative on average, or if the trigger does not improve hit rate relative to random dates.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation above target combined with unchanged policy rates raises real rate pressure; when CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays flat, equities should fall over the next month as discount rates and margin costs rise.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term is not negative in rolling regressions, or if periods of rising CPIAUCSL with flat FEDFUNDS do not show lower 20-day returns than either condition alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE without payroll deterioration indicates durable labor demand and resilient consumption, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.2% and PAYEMS trend nonnegative over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-unemployment and stable-payroll regime does not outperform unconditional returns over 60 days, or if returns are negative in more than half of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong sales growth in RSAFS, especially when paired with rising GDPC1, signals accelerating nominal demand and improves forward earnings expectations, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-GDPC1 signal fails to beat either proxy alone, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive when both series are trending upward.
